title: directory_user
description: One identity in any user directory — IdP (Okta, Azure AD), code platform (GitHub, GitLab), cloud IAM (AWS, GCP), or on-prem LDAP. The single cross-vendor shape policies use for MFA, admin, and lifecycle checks. Plugins normalize provider-specific status enums and MFA semantics into the boolean fields here; rules consume the same payload regardless of source.

id.description: Unique within the emitting source (AWS IAM UserId, Okta user id, GitHub login, LDAP DN, …).

display_name.description: Human-readable identifier (username, login, full name). Plugins populate the most useful single string for violation messages.

email.description: When known; not every directory exposes email.

mfa_enabled.description: True iff the identity has at least one MFA factor enrolled and required at sign-on. Plugins compute this from provider-specific MFA state.

mfa_factor_count.description: Number of enrolled MFA factors (optional, best-effort). Useful for richer rules; not every plugin can compute it.

is_admin.description: Has account-wide elevated privileges (IAM admin group, Okta SuperAdmin, GitHub org owner, …). Plugins implement the per-vendor heuristic.

is_service_account.description: Non-human identity (bot, machine, integration token). Plugins use vendor-specific signals; absent means unknown.

is_active.description: Account can authenticate today. Absent means assume active; explicit false skips the identity from active-only rules.
